profile.name: Marcus Chen
profile.tagline: Incident response and threat hunting in cloud-native environments. Focus areas: identity attacks, M365 forensics, and detection engineering.

cases.title: Business Email Compromise — CFO Account Takeover
cases.summary: AiTM phishing kit captured the CFO's session token post-MFA, granting adversary persistent Exchange access. A forwarding rule silently exfiltrated 14 emails before a fraudulent wire transfer of $127K was identified and blocked. Total dwell time: 6 hours. Detection-to-containment: 58 minutes.
cases.alert: Microsoft Sentinel — High Severity: "Impossible Travel: Sign-in from multiple geographic locations within 4-hour window" triggered at 02:14 UTC for cfo@[org].com. Sentinel fusion appended a co-occurring "MFA Fatigue: 7 consecutive push-notification denials" alert from the same session, escalating both indicators into a single P1 incident.

timeline.event: Sentinel fusion alert fires — Impossible Travel + MFA Fatigue correlated for cfo@[org].com. On-call analyst paged via PagerDuty.

Incident opened. IP-first triage: legitimate source 24.118.x.x (Austin, TX) vs. adversary source 185.220.101.47 (Bucharest, RO). AbuseIPDB returns 97% confidence malicious — confirmed active Tor exit relay.
VirusTotal and Shodan corroborate malicious classification. Entra sign-in logs show refresh token issued to adversary IP 6 hours prior (20:14 UTC) — pre-alert dwell confirmed.

Session ID pivot via Microsoft Graph API (auditLogs/signIns). Active refresh token confirmed still valid — adversary maintaining Exchange access without account password. Phishing domain credential-secure-login[.]com extracted from sign-in referrer field.

Exchange Online audit log surfaces inbox rule created at 20:31 UTC prior day: all mail matching wire, transfer, payment, or invoice forwarded to ext_forward_bec2024@proton.me and marked as read. Financial intercept confirmed.
Containment initiated: all active sessions revoked via Graph API (revokeSignInSessions). Conditional Access updated to require compliant device. MFA registration locked pending re-enrollment.
Malicious inbox rule removed. Full-content Exchange audit enabled. Exfiltration search confirms 14 emails forwarded over the 6-hour dwell window; subject lines reviewed for financial content.
Finance team notified. Wire transfer request sent from adversary at 01:47 UTC identified — $127,000 to external routing. Transfer still pending at receiving bank.
Bank fraud line contacted. Transfer flagged and blocked before settlement window closed. No financial loss incurred.
Post-incident timeline finalized. Detection gap documented: no existing rule for inbox rule creation on executive accounts. Lessons-learned session scheduled.

cases.rootCause: An AiTM (Adversary-in-the-Middle) phishing kit hosted at credential-secure-login[.]com operated as a reverse proxy to the legitimate Microsoft sign-in portal. After the CFO completed MFA, the kit captured the post-authentication session cookie and replayed it from a Tor exit node. Conditional Access did not re-evaluate device compliance on IP change. Legacy Continuous Access Evaluation (CAE) enforcement was absent from executive accounts, allowing the stolen token a 6-hour unrestricted access window.
cases.containment: All active sessions revoked via Microsoft Graph API (revokeSignInSessions), immediately invalidating the stolen refresh token. Conditional Access updated to enforce CAE strict-location mode for all C-suite accounts. Phishing domain submitted to Microsoft DART for tenant-level block. Malicious inbox rule removed; full-content mailbox audit enabled across C-suite. Finance team's wire approval process updated: all outbound transfers exceeding $10K require out-of-band phone verification with the requester before processing.

iocs.context: Adversary egress — Tor exit node, AbuseIPDB confidence 97%
AiTM phishing kit — reverse proxy to Microsoft O365 sign-in
Exfiltration mailbox — destination of malicious inbox forwarding rule
Stolen session refresh token — revoked 03:12 UTC

detections: KQL analytic rule: Alert on inbox rule creation targeting an external domain within 60 minutes of an Impossible Travel event for the same UPN. Severity: High. Auto-opens P1 incident.

KQL analytic rule: Correlate sign-in events where session token age exceeds 4 hours and the source country differs from token-issuance country. Alert enriched with UEBA risk score via Sentinel fusion.

Logic App playbook: On confirmed AiTM indicator (phishing domain in sign-in referrer OR Tor exit node IP), auto-invoke revokeSignInSessions and page on-call analyst — zero manual steps for initial containment.
Continuous Access Evaluation (CAE) enforced in strict-location mode for all Executive security group accounts. Token lifetime reduced from 60 minutes to 10 minutes with IP-binding enabled.
